# UC-22.3.47 · DORA Art.10 — Cisco ISE Multi-Site Replication Resilience Evidence

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** ITOps &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations

*We continuously evidence DORA Art.10 — multi-site ISE replication resilience. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.*

---

## Description

We continuously evidence DORA Art.10 — multi-site ISE replication resilience.

## Value

DORA Art.10 requires evidence of business continuity. Multi-site ISE replication is foundational for NAC continuity; this UC produces evidence.

## Implementation

Daily.

## Evidence

Saved search archived to `audit_evidence` with DORA Art.10 tagging.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

WAN flap impacts replication. Evidence captured.

### Negative scenario

Healthy replication. Empty result; control attested.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.61 operational.

## Step 1-5

```ini
[UC-22.3.47: DORA Art.10 — Multi-Site Replication]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.3.47,reg=DORA,clause=Art.10
```

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (37001, 37002, 37008) earliest=-1d
| stats count, max(replication_lag_seconds) as max_lag by site_pair, MessageCode
| eval dora_clause="Art.10", control="Multi_Site_BCDR"
| collect index=audit_evidence
```

## Visualization

DORA Art.10 panel: replication lag; WAN impact correlation.

## Known False Positives

1. Brief WAN flap during scheduled maintenance.

## References

- [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)
